reviews.review_description:
This game would be great but…
It doesn’t tell exactly how to pick your ideas for expeditions. So the beginning part of the game can be frustrating. Not to mention attacking enemies can be sloppy and doesn’t always read what you’re doing, especially with ranged weapons. 
All of that is fine and normal for VR games.
But the biggest red flag was when I had been playing for a long period of time, finally built up so good ideas all for the game to freeze mid expedition. I wait but it never fixes itself. So I rebut the game. All of my high level gear and weapons were gone. These weren’t weapons I received during the expedition they were weapons that I crafted that have been in my inventory for several expeditions all gone and now I need to start the crafting process over. Makes me want to uninstall the game.

Update.
I got new gear and beat the game. All in all it’s really fun. I wish there was more to do but after finishing the game I can say I did enjoy playing it and would recommend

developer_response.body:
Really appreciate you taking the time to share this, and that definitely sounds like a frustrating experience.

It sounds like you may have run into a bug with the game freezing and losing gear, and we’ll pass that along to the team to take a closer look. We also hear your feedback on the early expedition flow and combat feeling unclear.

In the meantime, you may be able to recover some of that lost gear by speaking with Quinn’s wolf in the kingdom, which can revisit the expedition where it was lost. Not ideal given what happened, but hopefully it helps a bit.

reviews.review_description:
I love this game! I just want more of it, it's exactly how you would imagine playing an old school dragon quest game in MR. The expeditions take place in your room and I felt like the old NES Zelda going screen to screen through the levels with the different events like fishing, mining, or battles, and the enemies and combat were like dragon quest only instead of turn based you can run around the room and have as much fun as you want with it. The bosses were really creative too, like dodging lasers while sniping out the eyes with your bow then slashing at the golem with your sword when he comes back out, so kool! I was so sad when I beat the last boss and I still kept playing for hours maxing everything out, and a month later I still go back an do an expedition or two constantly lol.

Please add more or make another game like this, it's really my favorite!!

developer_response.body:
This review is awesome, thank you for taking the time to leave it and for playing so much! I shared this with the team, it's always awesome to hear from people who love Mythic Realms :)

We've discussed doing some updates this year, but a lot depends on the sales numbers which right now aren't enough to support the development team focused on this sadly. Hopefully more people give MR a shot though, and see what kind of games you can make versus the tech demos most folks are accustomed to, and we can keep building out more content for you in Mythic Realms!

reviews.review_description:
This game is really fun, and so much more than what I thought it would be! I’m a little late to the party but absolutely loving the game! I am lvl 14 now and made it to the second place! The caves!!! So cool!
Edit:
My smithing guy won’t let me press a! I can’t upgrade my furnace! I have the lumber mill all the way upgraded! Sometimes the A isn’t even there, but the rest of the time it is there I just can’t interact with it at all!
Edit: I uninstalled it and reinstalled it and now it’s all good! 
Everyone should try this game

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review, and super glad to hear you're enjoying Mythic Realms so far! :)

The cave boss is my personal favorite, excited for you to see it!

reviews.review_description: I need more than one save profile; I want to play with my son, each of us in our own saved game, and I can't. The idea of ​​evolving the village in virtual reality and doing missions in mixed reality is very interesting; please add new exploration locations.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for feedback! We love hearing how you’re playing, especially with family. I’ll share your suggestion about multiple save profiles and additional exploration locations with the dev team!

reviews.review_description: Really enjoyed the game play. Love that it takes your room into the game and decorates accordingly. Gives a great mix of gathering and fighting with allowing the choice. Overall amazing game and would love more VR games like this!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review, and so glad to hear you enjoyed Mythic Realms!

We agree we'd love to see more games like this in VR too! :)

reviews.review_description:
Loved the game and wish there was more. 

My only complaint was the pacing felt off for the town vs the expeditions.. I'm not sure why it's the case, but the town stuff opened so slowly that I beat the final boss before I even got the first mission for melee mastery to be able to craft even the basic sword... Not sure what the trigger is to open that section of town and get that quest going earlier, but I looked for a while to no avail. 

That being said, you get so much loot from just playing the expeditions that you don't really need to craft much other than the gloves/shields, and even that isn't really too necessary if you just use the vampire buff haha

While I realize 90% of this review seems like complaining, the game really was 99% amazing! Loved adventuring in my living room and just wish there was more to experience or higher difficulty setting so that I can have a reason to work on crafting higher tier gear

I've been looking for a game like this since I was first introduced to Mixed Reality with the Pokemon Go craze a few years back. I just got a VR headset a month ago and this perfectly fills the thrill I've been missing since leaving Pokemon Go. Thank you to the developers for creating this adventure game I'm so happy I could slay a dragon!

developer_response.body:
This is awesome! Super glad that you're enjoying Mythic Realms :)

We also fell in love with Pokemon Go and how MR/AR can blend reality with virtual elements, the tech is really quite amazing!

reviews.review_description: The game scanned my room and everything was right, but the game thinks i'm taller so i cant open chests, and i have trouble killing slimes.
Super great it's so good and it's great that you can with turn your lost items

developer_response.body:
Glad to hear you're enjoying Mythic Realms, and thank you for the review! :)

We'd added the lost item purchase in the previous update, so really happy to hear you enjoy that feature.

reviews.review_title: The best MR game I’ve played.
reviews.review_description: Usually I don’t like MR games, but this one is great…It’s so simple but engaging in the best way. My space is JUST the recommended size, and I never felt cramped playing (first session I killed my battery), it really does feel like they take you somewhere else. Highly recommend.

developer_response.body: This is awesome, thank you so much for leaving this review! That was exactly what we were going for with Mythic Realms! (The taking your somewhere else that is, sorry about your battery!)

reviews.review_description:
So simple. So much fun. And yea break a sweat.
Just make sure you shut your dishwasher when it opens after the clean cycle finishes. Unless you enjoy getting yelled at by your wife… “You can’t play that inside!”

Aye caramba. I will be playing this as soon as my headset charges.

This game hooked me right away! I love the way my space is transformed and have to move around within it in order to interact and make choices on what to do and progress the
game. It has classic rpg elements with grinding, collecting, and quests to complete. Oh, yes, and slimes! The music and sounds fit perfectly. It is an excellent MR experience, one I'm so happy I jumped into.

Good game but the Physics are very basic
Swing hard or light barely changes anything. The town could be shrunk so not as basic and empty. Overall a good game - Birdman Vegan Future.

reviews.review_title: My go to game right now
reviews.review_description:
Grab this game if you haven't ladies and gents. It's such a fun unique experience. I always want to knock out one more run before stopping, which is always a good sign in gaming. Watching your gaming space transform into a myriad of different biomes is awe inspiring. kudos to whoever came up with the boss fight in the caves. It's secondary phase had my jaw literally dropped and it put the biggest smile on my face. 
Walking around my living room fishing, chopping down trees and fighting monsters never seems to get old. Definitely would recommend to anyone wanting a great polished MR experience.

The only 2 gripes I have is it gets a little easy once you level up your skills and collect enough gold to basically level up everything to max That buffs you significantly.  I also finished the second and third zones on level 10-12. So I have to heavily grind to get to 30 to fight the final boss. Maybe some tweaking and debuffs would fine tune these things. Lastly, you need just one more activity in the biomes, like maybe hunting for food. You already have the bow, just need to animate a deer or rabbit. Just a thought!!

Great freaking job guys!!!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the detailed review! It's awesome to see people enjoying the game :)

Appreciate the feedback on balance and more to do in expeditions as well! We definitely want to keep adding more activities during expeditions.

reviews.review_description: Doesn't  work

developer_response.body:
I'm sorry to hear it isn't working for you, if you'd like some support to get it working you can reach out to info@petricoregames.com or join the Mythic Realms discord here to ask the development team about how to get it working https://discord.com/invite/Cewyt7yrtY

We're here to help you get it working if you can provide more details on what is happening on your end!

reviews.review_description:
I really like this game, very simple mechanics, but it's charming and beautifully done.

Thanks devs

reviews.review_title: A lot of fun (with a few bugs)
reviews.review_description:
A really fun way to get a little excercise indoors. The game froze a few times, but restarting the game fixed it and I did not lose progress. 

I got a low memory error one time, so I think the game creates one or more memory leaks that need fixing.

All in all an excellent game and concept. I would like to see more area's in the future, so I kan keep building my character (maybe more towns with each their own set of area's?)

Edit: BEST END CREDITS EVER!! 😄 Seriously, amazing work!! 

Like others have said, this is one of the best use of MR and VR together on the market. I've played for about 3 hours and I already care about my villagers' well-being. I love the simple interactions with the npc's, the different weapons and gathering tasks. Great job!!
Nice idea guys! Cant wait for mor updates

When I first played this game I had a blast, then I went to my grandparents for the weekend and set up a new room, there was a couple bugs but nothing too serious. But when I had to re-set up the original room, it made me do the tutorial again and it was almost as if my save file was leaking into a new one. I still had all my quests, statues,weapons and even the passive rewards from the blacksmith and lumberyard. But it had locked all the levels and the npc’s were gone, also, it took away a blessing slot and my best blessing. If this can be fixed it’ll be a five star game for sure

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the thoughtful review and for flagging this issue! We’re really sorry you ran into that. We haven’t seen this exact bug before, but we really appreciate the detailed report. We’ll dig in and do some testing to try to replicate what you experienced and work on a fix. Glad to hear you’ve been enjoying the game otherwise, and we hope to earn that fifth star soon!

reviews.review_description: Ok I just finished the first boss dragon and wow this game is amazing! Combat is fun and the upgrading system is very promising at this early stage. Never thought I would be fighting dragons, slimes, and skeletons in my kitchen and living room!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We're thrilled to hear you're enjoying it :)

reviews.review_description: Among other games, I play beat saber at an expert+ level so I am not new to VR. But this was the first VR game I've played where I felt like I wasn't just coming up with an excuse to exercise or too motion sick to be immersed.  Well done!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review! :) 

We're secretly hoping that some day we get a "I got buff playing Mythic Realms" video on Youtube... :P

reviews.review_description:
The combo of VR+MR is really cool! It's fun to explore the different environments in your room and play the different minigames on your expedition. Combat is easily the most fun as you have so many different ways to play with the variety of weapons, not even in just range, melee and magic! Chests, NPCs and the Travelling Merchant allow you to gain lots of nice free stuff you can use on expedition or in the kingdom. Fishing is fun, Mining and woodcutting are more or less the same but utilize the same crit spot mechanic from combat, but now at your own pace!(Given it's not a mimic..)
The VR portion gives you access to the kingdom, which is your respite away from the active MR portion, allowing you to build up your kingdom and develop it overtime by unlocking the ability to craft and shop in the kingdom, rather than waiting to get free stuff on an expedition. You can also use the shrine and different decor spots to get upgrades so you can have an easier expedition!

Closest thing I can get to a RuneScape-ish type VR/MR game. I’m really impressed with how smooth it plays most of the time. That being said, you occasionally get a little glitch here and there like when you’re in the MR realm and an activity spawns inside my refrigerator or sometimes in my wall. It’s not all the time that it happens so I can get over it. For some reason I feel like we need a save button to save our progress, I guess it saves by itself but I just don’t fully trust that, but I guess it’s just a suggestion. Other than that, AMAZING! Keep doing you guys and take your time with the updates!!

Mythic realms is one of those games that just can't be replicated in the flat medium.
Its a hybrid MR/VR game that takes advantage of room scale.

Seeing monsters appear in your room to fight is one thing but having to walk your way around you room to do tasks is a whole different kettle of fish, entirely.

Tasks may be simple with you having to chop down trees, mine for ore and fish.
But having to carry them out in your living room and physically shows VR in a different light.

As for combat....its probably the strongest part of the game where you have to take advantage of hitting critical points and deflecting/blocking enemy attacks.

You have a range of melee/ranged and magic weapons at your disposal as you fight your way through skeletons, slimes, goblins and more.

The bosses are even more impressive in terms of scale, move sets and inventiveness

The gameplay loop is simple but very addictive as you level your character to deal more damage/take more and grind for materials to craft stronger weapons, gold to upgrade your village (granting you perks) 

Be warned its a rogue lite in that you lose your equipment when you die! So know when to quit a level (which pop up on milestone points in a stage)

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for playing and leaving us a review, we're thrilled to hear you enjoyed it! :)

reviews.review_title: Made me a Mixed Reality believer
reviews.review_description:
I’ve generally been lukewarm about Mixed Reality but Mythic Realms changed my mind. It has the perfect blend of MR modes/quests and VR world building that keeps everything fresh. The game performs fantastically on my Quest 3.

I did knock one star off as I feel that the variety of tasks during a quest is rather limited and can get repetitive after a few quests. I like the more advanced combat scenarios as you get deeper into quests, but having to fish/chop/mine in between the fun stuff can feel like work. More variety would make this an easy 5 star review!

I loved this game!
When first starting this game you are given the opportunity to pick your choice of weapon class, from Wizard, Archer and Knight and much like Pokémon Red where I chose Squirtle, I too here opted for the easiest beginner weapon and went with Wizard.
Now it's all academic as you will eventually unlock all other weapons later in the game, but in terms of ease of use I would say Wizard = easy, Archer = medium and Knight = hard for taking your first steps into the Mythic Realms.
Now during each run you have individual levels where you'll have 3 options to pick from, tree cutting, rock smashing and fishing are what will give you resources used for feeding the townsfolk and for crafting weapons.
Then there are other things that will be options periodically, such as using a campfire to regenerate health, a sword icon with arrow above to upgrade a weapon, a white figure sitting on a rock to get a free weapon and a backpack where you can buy items from a merchant.
The game switches from VR within the town to MR when you go on a run in one of the four realms, the realms appear all over your walls in forms of vines and leaves, to ice, lava, mushrooms and so many different colourful features, just keep an eye for any slivers of light where you'll be able to pick up a power up.
When you complete a realm by killing the boss in that area, you'll unlock the ability to add an upgrade, such as increased XP, increased gold to doing more damage and taking less damage, I think there are 8 to pick from and you'll get max 4 you can use at any one time.
Later in the game you'll be able to build a wood crafting shack to make weapons and a blacksmith to do the same and smelt metals.
One tip is follow the merchant and listen to everything they say, that's how you'll be able to unlock selling unwanted weapons and upgrading the buildings to make better weapons from higher class materials.
There's so many more things I could cover but this is already getting long enough for Peter Jackson to make a movie out of it, so I'll just say the game is a lot of fun, requires a decent sized play space (I was in around 2mX3m and it was about enough) and there's a great range of enemy types, bosses, weapons, honestly I got 8+ hours out of it and it's a total blast.
My one request from the devs would be to add co-op, how cool would it be if you had your own space and so does your friend with the rooms side by side and enemies able to move between and attack you both.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing and for the detailed review! We've got some updates coming soon to add even more to the game. While co-op isn't on the immediate roadmap, we have a few other things planned we're really excited about to share more info for soon! :)

reviews.review_description: …this game has potential, but some mechanics feel frustrating rather than fun. Fighting tough enemies forces you to spin your hand at extreme speeds to hit shots, which feels more exhausting than challenging. All the bosses makes you look straight up for too long, causing neck strain for some VR players. Teleporting between VR and Mixed Reality blasts you with a blinding white flash. NPCs lack personality, just wandering around making “bla bla” sounds, and the village feels empty despite its growth system. With better comfort, visuals, and engagement, this could be a great game, but right now, it needs work.

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR games i have tried.
reviews.review_description:
This is one of the best games i have tried on VR. 
The way the mixed reality feature of the game combines with normal VR is so smooth and it just works.
The only reason i gave this any less than 5 stars is because the portal you set in the start is where it stays for the entire game so if you set it in for example your bedroom and want more space for the mixed reality feature, there will be much walking back and forth.

reviews.review_title: Lots of fun, but crashes often.
reviews.review_description: I want to start off this review by saying the game is a lot of fun. I enjoy the loop of going out on expeditions and slowly building up gear and upgrading the town to get progressively stronger. But one thing that sours the mood more than anything is when I go out in some of my best gear available only for the game to freeze and cause me to lose all that equipment on that run. That sucks, and every time it happens, it sucks any and all enjoyment I was having from the game. Because the game feels the need to set back my progress with crashes after I spent so long crafting my gear, I cannot rate this higher than a 3/5

developer_response.body: Hello! I'm sorry to hear that you've encountered crashing while playing the game. Since you first wrote this review, we've launched two updates that should both help with performance, and hopefully stop the crashing that you encountered. I hope that you're able to update and give the latest version a try. Please let us know if you still encounter any issues on the latest version - and thank you for playing!

reviews.review_description:
The game is great overall! Fishing, mining, and chopping trees feel satisfying and well-designed. However, the combat leaves a lot to be desired. The sword lacks weight, phases through enemies, and there's no blocking or defensive mechanics—just rapid wrist flicking to deal damage, which doesn't feel engaging.

That aside, the game is still a fantastic experience, and I’d definitely recommend it!

I'm not afraid to admit that this game blew my mind.  I wasn't expecting much that I had not seen before with this genre.  But wait....this game has passthrough that allows you to play with a lot more safety and comfort through it's AR-like concepts through VR.  I was fishing in my lounge room.  Cutting down trees to gather logs to complete tasks, sword play, and a lot of interesting characters to engage with.  It's like a mythical and magical modern-day 'Oregon Trail', completing tasks in order to open up more of this fascinating world. As far as controlling go, I especially love the touch elements.

You don't need a massive space to play, but you do need enough light for maximum immersion.  The soundtrack is great too.

A decent mixed reality fantasy adventure, It does a great job of mixing between MR & VR.

The characters are fun and pretty impressive to fight against in room scale mode, the combat with the characters is impressive but could be better, and the Bosses are great. 

I think to bump up my star rating the story line could benefit from some narration, as I found when the speech bubbles came up the limited words used like “hmm” & “ha ha” did not sit right with me, just felt a little lacking in that department. 

It’s a pretty straight forward game, and has great characters and combat styles with weapon upgrades. 

Overall a promising title.

As soon as I setup this game, it was so cool to see how the environment was  mapped throughout the room.  One corner of my room had tress to be chopped down. While playing this game it brought my inner kid smile to my face. I love how interactive and fun the game mechanics were.  The only thing I wish there was more of was a variety of different locations to the battles, at times it felt very repetitive. I would highly recommend this game to those who want a fun and interactive mixed reality game.

Mythic Realms has got to be one of my favorite games to play. I really enjoy gathering resources to build different structures in game. Fighting the monsters is what makes the game thrilling with all of the verities there are. When playing it is pretty cool to fight the monsters anywhere in your place of living. The mixed reality is really smooth from being in the town to the mission. Overall I really enjoy this game and I do recommend that should play this game.

This game has gotten a lot of things right just to start with. The mixed reality does not feel gimmicky at all and still adds detail to your area to keep it from being bland. The leveling and quest system is usually top tier(I'll go into more detail later) the quests are easy to find and accept and they give a decent amount of rewards towards progressing your village which appears to be the ultimate goal. The combat is pretty good and self explanatory, there are weak spots on enemies that are highlighted and deal more damage and you will have to use a combination of dodging and precision if you hope to prevail. The story could have been a bit more in-depth but it is easy enough to follow and not annoying. The one main thing I didn't like about this game and which is why I gave it four stars, this may be a quest 2 issue only though, after doing a quest for a while occasionally I would be locked into the map unable to escape or do anything else leading to me having to leave and lose my progress for that specific area and time. Besides the issues that can be fixed most likely I would recommend this game to anyone looking to experiment with mixed reality or someone who already has experience but just doesn't feel like you've used its full potential. Even if you don't entirely want it because of the mixed reality again as I said it's not just a gimmick selling point and the game is good without mixed reality but mixed reality just adds to it.
Oh wow, such a fun experience. There is a MR and VR part of gameplay, but I like the mixed reality wayy more. Really a great use of the technology! 100% recommended!

Mythic realm is a simple but addictive MR/VR - RPG. Your aim is to do "expeditions" right in your living room to gather ressources, fight nasty monsters and get lots of loot. You can spend those ressources to level up your gear, build and upgrade "your" town and complete sidequests. The basic concept is simple but the gameplay loop is entertaining and addictive. 

I especially enjoyed the townbuilding part - The graphic style reminded me a lot of the classic browser RPG runescape which I cherish a lot - Childhood memories came back.

The expeditions were a lot of fun - stuff like chests which turn out to be a mimic or bosses occur. The MR part of the game turned out really well. The game did a great job to turn my living room into a dungeon with some decorative elements. When you advance further into the game and unlock other dungeon types my living room got "redecorated" several times. I was having a blast while fighting the skelleton infestation behind my couch. 

For the price the game is a steal and I recommend it fully.

reviews.review_title: A unique concept with a great execution
reviews.review_description:
Mythic Realms is an ambitious title combining a roguelite adventure in Mixed Reality with a kingdom that you can upgrade in virtual reality. And ambitious as it may be, Mythic Realms completely delivers.

With RPG elements such as skill progression, quests, various weapons, and upgrades to your town - combined with roguelite elements that keep you coming back - Mythic Realms is a gem on the Meta Quest store.

Mystic realms is my favorite RPG game I love the mixed reality and that fact that it brings my living room alive . This game will have you making your own kingdom with awesome graphics, great enemies and tasks that will keep you on your toes .as you fight your way through the enemies while keeping your kingdom safe and building it up and having great adventures all while having the time of your life. As you go through the levels you build up your skills and weapons while also helping people in your kingdom.when you go on quests you get better gear to help you with the quests . It's cool that you can play as a kinght , ranger or mage . Ultimately the choice is yours which ever one you choose your sure to have a great time playing

reviews.review_title: Good try, but not for me
reviews.review_description:
I really liked the concept of the game, but it wasn't for me. It is a very cool idea to have a mix of VR and MR in a quest/dungeon crawl game, but playing in my house didn't feel fantastic enough.

The MR questing is an interesting idea, but it's too repetitive. You are playing in the same, familiar space all the time and the small thematic changes aren't enough to make it feel new. The quest activities weren't engaging enough to maintain my interest in what is a small variation of my everyday world.

The town building, which is in full VR, shows some real promise. As you progress through the game you are introduced to different characters who usually want to build something. After you supply them with what they need, they will add a building to the town, which enhances the game. Unfortunately, while this is a very nice part of the game, the part of the game I want to play most is still the questing.

I've seen some Youtubers complain about the graphics, but I really liked the art style. The character design is good and they aren't just the same shape and scale with different hair styles. Some characters are tall hulking dudes, and then there's the short, squat, old wizard with some variance in between. OK, they are fairly cliche, but I'm not looking for fine art or diversity here. I think they look good. As for the game in general, the town looks nice. I do wish the different levels affected the look of MR more effectively or that I found the quests to be more engaging.

Super fun mixed reality game! 

The graphics make the appartment look so cool, for example a sky where the ceiling was, Grass, little Mushrooms or Gems on the Ground and much more, all of that with nice Graphics on my Quest 3. 

The Gameplay itself also makes me want to play again and again until now, I always want to explore everything, get stronger, build this or that and so on. 

U really need space, in my small appartment I fear a bit breaking things, especially later maybe if it get's harder. 

But if u have enough space and nothing bad happens it may be one of these games where u have the feeling u had before trying so many VR-Games, at least that's what happened to me. 

Although Mixed Reality isn't totally new this game feels fresh for me and ais, it's really fun! 

Only that some people might break things, injure themselfes, other people, pets or something like that might be a problem, so do it on ur own risk, play save and have fun! 😎👍

And a Tipp: In the Beginning u can choose a weapon, don't just grab any, think about what fighting style u might want for a longer while, cause although u are able to fight with other weapons it might be an advantage to choose the class u really want in some ways. 

All of that said in the current state while I'm posting it.

Mythic Realms delivers an enchanting MR/VR experience that brilliantly blends community-building and adventure. My favorite moment was when I finally grasped the game mechanics, allowing me to witness the village flourish, which filled me with a sense of accomplishment. The vibrant art style and whimsical graphics create an immersive atmosphere, making exploration delightful. The seamless transition between VR and mixed reality adds an exciting layer to gameplay, enhancing the overall adventure. Each level offers unique challenges that keep you engaged and motivated to progress. With the potential for future updates introducing new environments and quests, Mythic Realms promises to be a captivating journey that invites players to dive into its magical world!

reviews.review_title: A pain the butt to set up and start
reviews.review_description: I own about 300 games and this game takes the cake as far as being the worst experience to set up.  After the setup that finally started and the game was solid but each enemy took a million hits to kill and didn’t have enough enemies fighting at once.  I hate fishing and chopping trees isn’t my thing.  The hub world seemed a little confusing and empty. I wish I could just upgrade in MR.  The hub takes you out of the immersion

reviews.review_title: Well-made RPG with a unique MR spin.
reviews.review_description:
Love this game. Am lvl 31 now and still upgrading my skills to kill the boss in the 3rd scenario.
Fun mix of a VR game where you pickup quests and craft items and an MR game where you fight monsters and gather resources in your living room.
Lots of different gear and weapons to choose from. Melee, ranged, magic. You can play the way you like.
And the latest patch makes it way clearer what skills you need to level up to be able yo use specific gear.
Been playing this game daily for a few weeks now. Kudos to the team!
Request: the game needs save slots so I can let others try it.

Mythic Realms ambitiously blends roguelite gameplay with both VR (town activities) and MR (expeditions), bringing fantasy combat and exploration directly into your real-world space. One of its biggest strengths is its movement-based gameplay—dodging attacks by physically maneuvering feels far more immersive than traditional joystick movement.

Beyond expeditions, town progression, upgrades, and leveling add meaningful rewards and replay value. MR works best in simple room setups, and boss battles feel cool when the system functions as intended. However, MR sometimes feels more like a novelty than a fully integrated mechanic, and its benefits beyond free movement aren’t always clear.

The game still has rough edges, with missing choices during expeditions sometimes forcing mid-run restarts and causing frustrating progress loss. Some of these issues may stem from MR tool limitations and complex play spaces. Combat, while fun, leans toward attack spamming, and enemy AI could use more variety to make encounters more strategic.

With recent Meta Quest API improvements, future updates could refine environment scanning to prevent awkward enemy spawns and improve MR’s interaction with real-world objects (this could be really cool). 

Despite some growing pains, Mythic Realms is an engaging, forward-thinking roguelite. Fans of fantasy RPGs, roguelites, and those curious about mixed reality will find plenty to enjoy. With continued updates, it has the potential to become a standout MR experience—and despite its imperfections, I had a lot of fun playing.

reviews.review_title: You need some good MR play space.
reviews.review_description:
Absolutely amazing game, held back by forced mixed reality. Medieval RPG with roguelike dungeons, varied weapons, resource collection, quests, town building, and crafting is fantastic. Combat is engaging, and boss fights are great. However, needing a large 2mx2m+ space is frustrating. My limited space (5ftx8ft, furniture-filled) makes combat difficult. MR elements, like items on furniture and wall-based secrets, are cool, but some objects are too large to reach, and enemies force wall-bouncing to dodge. This feels restrictive and risks collisions.

A VR-only option would make this a 5-star game. Town navigation in VR is fine, but combat requires excessive movement, which is problematic in tight spaces. Other issues: non-adjustable UI, limited settings (no speed control), unclear or missing tutorials, and a single save slot. Starting weapon choices impact NPCs, but despite getting other weapons, to see the other NPCs it requires a full restart.

If you have ample MR space, it's a fun RPG/roguelike. I hope they add a VR-only mode or I get a larger play area.

I was a tester for this game. If it wasn't for that, this is a title I would never have gave the time of day.

With that being said, I was drawn immediately into it's world. It starts off slow and then unexpectedly out of nowhere I'm battling a skeleton in my real life personal space.

I'm pretty sure I was giggling at times because of the fun I was forced to have.

It progresses slowly but at a pace that keeps you wanting more and excited for the next page you turn.

This is definitely one of those games that deserve a look and you might be surprised like I was.

Thanks to the team that made this game possible and much love for opening my gaming vision to tmnew play styles 🍻

reviews.review_title: Adventures from your own living room!
reviews.review_description:
In Mythic Realms, you are playing as a hero who goes into fantastical expeditions.
Each expedition takes place in mixed reality, where you get to collect resources, find treasure, and fight monsters from within your own living room. 
The mixed reality works really well and it can be impressive at times to see the monsters showing up inside of your house.

After and expedition has finished, you will go back to the main town, which is actually in full virtual reality. Jumping back and forth between VR and MR is pretty cool, and the story weaves it all together flawlessly.

This is a pretty fun game, and there's plenty to do to keep you busy for a while.

I love games like this so it's perfect for me. Had a lot of fun playing, just wish there were more magic and weapon type maybe even elemental weapons. If possible multi-player would make the game a lot better and tweak movement motions and bow a bit to make it work better. But so far the game has been amazing and a must try for me.

This game is honestly so fun, and it really is MADE for VR. Exploring dungeons in my own house is exciting and unique. The NPCs are funny and engaging! The fighting mechanics weren't perfect, but I still had a great time fighting slimes, skeletons, and mimics all in my living room. This game is super replayable and has so much room for expansion; super excited to see what they do next.

Everything I beat the 3 realm boss .the game it's stuck..there is no cutscene..
And only the boss corpse Dan laying down on the floor...I had to quit the game .but still I need to start over again the third realm...and same things happen

developer_response.body:
Oh no, I'm sorry to hear that you're running into an issue with the game. We could use a little more info about what is going on though, so we can investigate the bug.

If possible, could you please join our discord to share more info about what is happening? Our developers are on there and this will be really helpful to investigate the issue.

Link to Join: https://discord.gg/Cewyt7yrtY

reviews.review_description:
I just stopped playing the game. And here's why: Selecting the category in menus (worksbenches, equipment planning at the mission board) works very well. In contrast to selecting weapons or armor in menu. I tried to select something, and it failed at least five times, without exaggeration. Only the later one was the input registered. Since this is a frequent action, for example, comparing weapons with other weapons, I find it very annoying and it severely hinders the flow of the game. Currently, I would give the game  three stars and hope that it will be improved.
PS: After the latest patch the ui feels improved, i increase to 5 Stars.
Now just an improved way to select a weapon or tool during an expedition instead of just switching through everything with one button. Would be cool.

Actually pretty good. Combat is good, boss fights are good, music doesn’t get annoying. Only gripe is the forge. The sound of tipping metal into the fire is nightmarish, and the hammer mini game has bad hit registration. Feels like it has room to grow, will change to 4 * if good updates.

developer_response.body: Hello! Sorry about the sound with the furnace making it in for launch, it's since been fixed in a recent update to be much less annoying! On top of that we just released a new hotfix today with more fixes, and plan to add more content in an upcoming May update. Hope that you can check it out! :)

reviews.review_description: I like this game and how it works with the surrounding area and clutter in your room. But even with limited space because of e.g. furniture it works really well. When I saw a dragon flying over my head (there was no ceiling anymore) I was hooked. The game is basically split in two games, one part is talking to NPCs in "your" town, where you get quests and rebuild the town (with money and items from the "dungeons". You can oftentimes choose between two or threee different things you can do while you are in a run, from fishing to chopping trees (with some surprises eventually) and obviously fighting enemies. You might also encounter NPCs on your way, helping you out with a new weapon for free or with items from their store. As with most roguelikes, you will lose most items when you die, but there are chances to leave a run when you're past some waves, and chose so. The low-poly artstyle fits well and the performance of the game was good. I played a pre-release version of the game though and have to return to it again.

reviews.review_title: A Living Room Adventure Like No Other!
reviews.review_description:
Ever dreamed of transforming your living room into a battleground of mythical proportions? Mythic Realms makes that dream a reality! This mixed-reality fantasy RPG blends the thrill of roguelite exploration with the immersive power of VR, bringing treacherous forests, icy caves, and epic boss battles right into your own space.

🛡️ Adventure Awaits… at Home!
From the moment I stepped into Mythic Realms, I was hooked. Watching my surroundings morph into a vibrant, interactive world where creatures hide in the walls and furniture becomes part of the battlefield was pure magic. Every expedition offers something new — whether I’m battling hordes of enemies, gathering resources, or even taking a quiet moment to fish. It’s a delightful blend of action and exploration that keeps me coming back.

👑 Save the Kingdom!
Between adventures, I returned to my growing kingdom, where a cast of charming NPCs awaited. These characters add a lot of personality to the game, offering quests that help build up your gear and expand your empire. It’s satisfying to see your progress reflected in both your loot and your kingdom’s development.

⚔️ Combat That Keeps You on Your Toes
The mixed-reality combat is where Mythic Realms truly shines. Enemies aren’t confined to a fixed space — they can scale your furniture, hide around corners, and ambush you when you least expect it. You’ll need to stay on your toes and use your room to your advantage to outwit these magical foes. The variety of weapons is impressive too — whether you prefer the precision of a bow, the raw power of a sword, or the magical finesse of a wand, there’s a playstyle for everyone.

🛠️ A Few Bugs, But Devs Are On It
As with many ambitious VR experiences, there are still some minor bugs here and there. Occasionally, tracking glitches or unexpected interactions can pull you out of the immersion, but the good news is that the developers are actively addressing these issues. They’ve been incredibly attentive to player feedback

reviews.review_title: My Living Room Is Now a Dungeon
reviews.review_description:
You know a game’s good when you’re chopping down trees next to your laundry basket and it feels epic.

Mythic Realms turns your house into a fantasy battleground, and somehow, it works. One minute you’re fishing in your hallway, the next you’re fighting a slime that just popped out of your coffee table. It’s a mixed reality roguelite where you gather resources, upgrade your village, and fight increasingly ridiculous monsters; all while trying not to trip over your dog.

The combat is fun if occasionally a wrist workout (note: if you're a Knight, stretch first). Magic and ranged attacks feel solid, bosses are massive and dramatic, and the town-building loop is surprisingly satisfying. Losing all your gear when you die stings, but hey, that's the roguelite life.

A few nitpicks: melee could use more weight, the NPCs have the personality of potatoes, and if your play space is small, good luck grabbing that potion behind your bookshelf. Still, it’s got heart, it’s got humor, and it makes your living room way more interesting.

Recommended. Just maybe clear some floor space first.

A fantastic little adventure that takes you in and out of mixed reality. The graphics are stylish and fun, combat feels good and is addictive, and the lure of gathering resources as you build your virtual village will keep you coming back. 

Definitely worth checking out!  This one shows what happens when mixed reality is more than just a gimmick

Mythic realms are using your roomscale so you can fight monsters in your own house! The art style isn't really what I prefer, but I know some people will definitely like it. 

This game is a first of its kind in the VR/MR experience, which they implemented really good into VR.

The game is really good fishing, mining, and chopping down trees is fun and feels good but the combat is really not that good, sword has no weight to it phases through enemies and no blocking no nothing your just flicking your wrist fast to deal damage it just isn’t fun to play with. Other than that this game is really good and I would recommend it.

This game is a lot of fun. It's a VR/AR game which will use you space to create a dungeon (expedition). You will be fighting and gathering resource, doing quest, each time you go out. The gameplay loop of going out to improve your equipment and town is really satisfying and is making me come back to play more and more!

This is possibly the best implementation of switching between full VR and MR. 

The game idea is great as well as its implementation. You upgrade / level up your village in virtual reality and fight monsters in mixed reality in your room. Which makes for a very unique and fun time. 

The controls are very easy to grasp, i love games that you dont need a tutorial to play. It helps if you do it, but the game really is straightforward. New things that were introduced that you needed to learn was smoothly implemented with a NPC teaching you or showing you were to go during gameplay. I thought that worked well and very clever, kept me immersed in the experience.

The game is generally easy to play and grasp, but it doesnt mean the game doesnt get difficult. The battles and quests feels very good and well balanced.

If you like sword fighting or beat em ups, this is definitely satisfying. There isnt just close ranged fighting classes theres also mage and archer which i could imagine are a little less exhausting. So given that i think many different type of players would enjoy it. 

The only negative thing i could say is that its low poly style and textures are not my favourite style. But it does help the experience be amazingly smooth, bold and simplistic. For what it is and needs to do, it definitely works well. 

Looking forward to more well put together experiences from this dev.
This game is really good and addictive, but I'm not too sure how to upgrade the structures, like the blacksmith requires level 2 furnace to get certain metal ingots.

developer_response.body: Thanks! You can upgrade the buildings by talking with each of the shopkeepers for each building. They'll have an option to upgrade. Hopefully you can craft some awesome gear now :)

reviews.review_title: The best execution of an MR game!
reviews.review_description:
In this game you play in both VR and MR.
 In the VR portion of the game, you are in a village that has been “attacked”. After you gather resources, you give them to villagers and see your town grow (you can actually see more people as you get more villagers). There, you can also upgrade your character and craft new equipment.
In the MR portion of the game, you see your room become part of the world. You fight enemies that have different abilities and fighting styles. You also fight bosses in the middle and at the end of the levels. The only downside is that you need a big room to experience the game in its full glory.
You have different classes to choose from, each with its own fighting style. You have two long-range options (bow and magic staff) and two close-range options (swords and two-handed swords). One good thing is that you are not forced to play with the class you chose at the start of the game. During the game, you get weapons from other classes that you can use. 
The main game loop is you going to the dungeons in MR and exploring. During your exploration, you can choose your own path, from mining ores to chopping wood, fishing, and even encountering wandering merchants. You can also choose to fight in order to get gold (aka money), but be careful, if you die, you lose all the weapons you get during your adventures.

If you want to play a proper MR roguelite this is the game for you. With very fun combat where you can face enemies in your room and gather loot and fighting bosses. The game is currently in early access so updates will be coming out but I experience no game breaking bugs and only a few minor issues that didn't even break my immersion. The main bug I had was in one section of a fight a enemy stopped taking damage from my magic cause it was glitching into the rock for the dungeon design, but I was able to still damage it by hitting it with my staff. This game has been very fun and I'm really hoping that an endless dungeon mode gets added. If you're interested in running around your room killing monsters and you're ok with a few bugs from early access this is a fun game for you.

The game is so buggy you can’t finish the tutorial (the teleporter is nowhere to be found).
The combat is one of the worst you can find. No parrying, no sense of weight.
Don’t believe the ai generated fake review. Also, no possibility to get a refund, so beware this 5 min “tech demo” that pretends to be a game.

developer_response.body: Hello! I'm sorry to hear you encountered a bug with the teleporter. We just released a hotfix update today that should fix this issue for you. When you get a chance, can you please update your game and try on the new version? Let us know if this now works for you, and we really appreciate you playing and giving us this feedback on the bug you encountered.

reviews.review_title: A Great Mix Of MR and VR!
reviews.review_description: Highly recommend to get a big playspace since this game takes full advantage of it! It fully immersed me into the "fantasy" world, which is my Room... but with dragons!! The monsters are nice and balanced. The graphics are fun and I like the character designs! Only somewhat con is that the combat is not for me. I'm sure someone out there will like it much more than me though! Highly recommend for people like me who used to be a normal creative kid and imagined a fantasy world in your room, this is made for you!!

reviews.review_title: Epic Adventures Await in Your Home
reviews.review_description:
This game completely blew me away! The mixed-reality mechanics transform your living room into a magical battlefield, immersing you in treacherous forests and icy caves without ever leaving home. Playing as a ranger, I was captivated by the combat—enemies climbing over my furniture and hiding behind walls felt thrillingly real. The roguelite gameplay had me hooked, constantly coming back for "just one more run," whether upgrading my bow or experimenting with new weapons like shurikens.

The kingdom-building aspect adds depth; meeting quirky NPCs and upgrading gear made every expedition feel rewarding. Fishing by my couch or battling epic bosses in my dining room added a sense of wonder that’s hard to describe.

This game is perfect for adventure-seekers who want VR to push boundaries. Mythic Realms is polished, inventive, and endlessly fun. Whether gathering loot or outsmarting foes, it’s a game worth diving into!

It's one of the first games I have played that fully utilizes mixed reality and is its own game and not just a game mode. I've seen some other ones, but they're mostly just shooters. It was a very fun experience, and I very much enjoyed it. I think as long as they add more things like weapons and levels and just more improvements overall, this game will be great for a long time and hold its own. I will be playing this game until I beat it and even more when new stuff gets added.

I got to try an early-access release of the game, and I was impressed. The combat can be difficult (I prefer a crossbow or staff paired with a shield so I can simply Become Turret, but other weapons have other mechanics), and the rest of the game is quite relaxing. Fun art style, audio can be distracting from the experience at times, and really cool mixture of VR and MR. Had some bugs, but the devs are dedicated and recently released a patch to fix it up. Would recommend, especially if you like leveling up skills, practicing with different combat mechanics, and battles where you actually move around the room to evade and make attacks!

You’re obviously interested in MR games and RPG’s if you’re checking this out. Does no disappoint. Very neat n clever game, fight some bad guys in the living room, try and fish, chop down trees, get some supplies to help this town thrive, and unlock new areas of town etc. 
havnt played that deep into it, couple of hours. 
But glad I got it. 
Would recommend

This game is very-well made. It was a lot of fun to complete tasks in my room, traveling from one end to fish and to the other end to cut down trees growing through my bed. There are quite a few tasks and occasions to be fully immersed in VR to build up the town. Be prepared to physically move around a lot because the game takes advantage of all of the space in your room. 
If you enjoy mixed reality games, you won’t want to miss this one.

If you're looking for a fresh and unique experience, this might be the one. Mythic Realms is an adventure roguelike which presents limitless replayability to those who enjoy boundless excitement. This experience features you selecting from three weapons when you begin and using them to battle monsters and become stronger.  With its blend of mixed reality and roguelike elements, Mythic Realms promises an experience that will keep you engaged for hours on end.

I'm not a huge fan of ar/Mr games but this one works well and the gameplay loop is addictive and fun. It has elements of a rogue like as well as adventuring and city building.  You can go to 4 different realms that have a number of activities such as fishing and gathering resources. You can pick several different weapons and classes. The battles are fun and it's nice to build up the town area. The only issue I have is sometimes the enemies are in the walls and the mobs spawn too close.  If you like adventure games I'd definitely recommend this game to you it is a lot of fun.

Mythic Realms mixes MR and VR seamlessly where you battle enemies with medieval weapons. This game can be hard with a small play space as when you fight enemies, you have to physically dodge their attack by moving out of the way. The weapons come from all different types of classes like melee, range and so much more. You can fight off skeletons, dragons, etc. This game really takes control of MR and how cool it can be in games. You should definitely try it.

This game is way more fun than I expected.  

The character development and RPG gamestyle is especially good (and addictive) where you can play in quests (in mixed reality), the graphics and art style is really nice and vibrant.

I always felt pleasantly surprised as I encountered new foes and characters.  The artwork and 3D for mixed reality is excellent.

The roomscale features worked good in my "modestly sized" room and there is nothing like having a dragon breathe fire into your space :)

For early access they have really nailed it where you have a mixed reality RPG where you need to physically play in your space to progress, it is a really good game mechanic.

Sure the game has things it can improve on, but this is a really cool game that I think would be fun and easy for everyone to play and I am looking forward to more game updates from the Dev Team.

reviews.review_title: Great game fun and decently polished!!!
reviews.review_description: The game has a unique feature where it swaps from vr to mr repeatedly its super smooth and the game has dungeon like mechanics and even a few cooler features like chopping trees it dosent have to many bugs and is very enjoyable. I highly reccomend to people new to vr wanting to test out mixed reality and virtual reality!!! Looking forward to seeing where this game goes!

First, what a fresh style of gameplay! To be able to mix mixed reality with virtual reality and make it work so cohesively is a feat of its own. This deserves praise first and foremost! 

This unique adventure game takes you on a journey, unlike most that have come out, within your own play area! Which was really fun and different, in a good way. 

Being able to choose which task needed to be completed gave your adventure a sense of freedom and strategy to assist your town as it grows. 

Without giving away too much, the grind makes you want to keep coming back, leveling up and completing the adventures in different areas that need to be accomplished. 

This is a fresh new take on an adventure game within your play space, utilizing MR and VR in a way that makes it easy and enjoyable for anyone new to VR all the way to VR enthusiasts.

This game feels magical!  One moment you are in VR then it takes you to your home in MR decorated in activities to do such as fighting enemies and gathering resources.  This is a very creative idea.  With these resources you build up your VR town.  This game will get you moving.  I hope in the future more realms will be created and perhaps you can build a town with other players.  I hope this game turns into a much bigger game in the future because the idea of it deserves it.  You should definitely check it out if you like town building and fighting games!

reviews.review_title: One of the best mixed reality games
reviews.review_description:
I have been really enjoying this one. Its kind of a dungeon crawler but you play in your own room. If its not too small it will actually give you alot of freedom since you can move around without worrying about hitting something. Fight s become really dynamic this way, especially with special moves from bosses. Speaking of, i like how bosses are present as larger enemies beyond your room, giving it space to move around but also retreat for in between phase waves.
Fights itself, while quite spammy, do give skilled players an edge with weak points to attack in random positions and not just a fixed body part like the head. The enemies are also varied with their own set of moves and designs. Overall theyre pretty good (except that phantom thing in the ground, i DONT like you!)

Next to combat you can do different little things in a run, like gathering resources or meeting NPCs. Since everything is in your room, its more like taking turns on what to do next rather than an actual dungeon, but i would be asking for the impossible in a mixed reality game.

Beyond doing dungeon runs, you have a town in VR that you can kind of upgrade with shops and some stat boosting statues. Its the weaker part of the game but also just there to spend your money and gear up for another round. I appreciate that you can both just buy and find gear, but also craft it with a kinda fun crafting mechanic rewarding you for being fast.

Overall the game is pretty nice and except for maybe a bit more town customization, i barely have anything to complain about.
